ops.title: Correct token accounting from Claude Code transcripts — usage dedup rule, workflow-agent file locations, sidechain gotchas
ops.body:
Measuring real billed tokens from Claude Code transcripts (via `cc-transcript show --json`):

- **Dedup rule**: consecutive assistant events sharing an identical `usage` object are blocks of ONE API message (parallel tool calls interleave their results between the block lines). Count usage once per message; per-event summing overcounts 3-4×.
- **Workflow agents live at `<session>/subagents/workflows/wf_*/agent-*.jsonl`** — a non-recursive `subagents/*.jsonl` glob misses them (in the 2026-07 audit: 3,457 files ≈ 41% of all spend).
- **Subagent files mark every event `is_sidechain: true`** — any analysis gated on `not is_sidechain` silently zeroes fork-lane metrics.
- Hook payloads live in `other:attachment` events (`attachment.type: hook_*`), not in grep-able text; `meta.is_compact_summary` marks compaction; cache tier is 1h ephemeral corpus-wide (writes bill 2×, not 1.25×).
- Cost model used: in ×1, out ×5, cache_read ×0.1, cc_1h ×2 (input-token-equivalents).
- Reusable scripts from the audit session (scratchpad, session 6528c1f3): `analyze_session.py`, `sweep_corpus.py`, `aggregate.py`. See [[token-wastage-audit-2026-07]].
